full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Decide which two components to ablate
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: For TC-GMM, if I have to ablate two distinct components of the method, what are the two most informative things to remove? I'm thinking: (1) the Historical GMM / model order selection, and (2) temporal coherence of data order. Are these distinct enough?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed these are independent components (one is algorithmic, one is a data assumption) and suggested the shuffling trick for ablation 2.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified they are covered in separate sections of the paper (2.3 vs 2.1) and represent independent design choices.

top_5_prompts.prompt: For TC-GMM, if I have to ablate two distinct components...
top_5_prompts.why_important: Helped me confirm that removing temporal ordering (by shuffling) is a valid ablation of the TC assumption rather than a data artefact.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Part B.
